name: Christopher Ruckman
tagline: Senior Software Engineer & Systems Architect specializing in multi-tenant SaaS platforms, microservice ecosystems, and cloud-native ERP development.

skills.content: Microservices, multi-tenant SaaS design, event-driven systems, API gateway routing, domain-driven design, modular UI composition, cloud cost optimization.
ASP, ASP.NET (VB.NET, WebForms), ASP.NET Core (C#, MVC, Razor, Blazor), RESTful APIs, React, Next.js, Node.js, Kotlin, TypeScript/JavaScript.
AWS EC2/RDS/S3/CloudFront/EventBridge, Docker, GitHub Actions CI/CD, Nginx reverse proxy, environment-based configuration, observability pipelines.
Entity Framework Core, SQL Server, PostgreSQL, FastAPI, Python, Express, distributed logging, background workers, event publishing/consumption.
Blazor Server/WASM, React, responsive UI systems, component-driven design, dynamic module loading, theme engines.
XUnit, Moq, integration testing, structured logging (Serilog), peer code reviews, CI-based validation.
UI/UX prototyping, Adobe Photoshop, Blender, branding systems, documentation design.

summary: Senior software engineer and systems architect specializing in multi-tenant SaaS platforms, microservice ecosystems, and cloud-native ERP design. I build scalable architectures, resilient backend services, and expressive interfaces that support real-world business operations across multiple industries. Recent work includes architecting Business As Usual (BAU), a full-service ERP platform with 40+ domain modules, event-driven workflows, tenant-per-database isolation, and a cost-optimized AWS deployment model running under $100/month.

items: Authored a three‑book series exploring resilience and personal transformation, including original cover design, thematic branding, and grassroots marketing.
Investigated litigation and consumer-targeted fraud, authored formal reports, and collaborated with regulatory agencies to support case integrity and public accountability.

Reinforced decades of engineering experience with modern, self‑directed mastery across cloud architecture, microservices, mobile development, and UI frameworks to remain current, relevant, and highly effective in today’s software landscape.

experience.title: Senior Software Engineer & Systems Architect
details: Architecting Business As Usual (BAU), a full-scale multi-tenant SaaS ERP platform with 40+ domain modules spanning Financials, HR, Operations, CRM, Inventory, Logistics, and industry-specific verticals.
Designed a microservice ecosystem with tenant-per-database isolation, event-driven communication, dynamic module discovery, and a unified API gateway.
Built a cost-optimized AWS deployment model (EC2, RDS, S3, CloudFront, EventBridge) running under $100/month while supporting dozens of independently deployable services.
Developed Admin/Main applications using Blazor and MVC with dynamic micro-frontend loading, theme engines, and runtime module composition.
Implemented CI/CD pipelines using GitHub Actions → S3 → CodeDeploy, enabling zero-downtime deployments and contributor-friendly workflows.
Building BAU Mobile: native Android and iOS applications consuming the same gateway-driven microservice architecture.
Developed NEXTRiff, a React/TypeScript + FastAPI platform for music tab creation, parsing, and playback with a modular backend and cloud asset pipeline.

experience.title: Android Developer
details: Delivered production Android features using Kotlin/Java, including Chromecast SDK integrations and scalable MVVM-based components.
Collaborated in enterprise engineering workflows with code reviews, unit testing, and iterative feature delivery.
Improved app stability and performance through lifecycle-aware components and modular architecture patterns.

experience.title: Software Developer
details: Built and maintained ASP.NET MVC and WebForms applications supporting operational workflows, reporting, and internal business processes.
Implemented and consumed REST APIs backed by SQL Server, improving data accessibility and system reliability.
Modernized legacy modules and improved maintainability through incremental refactoring and UI updates.

experience.title: Full-Stack Developer
details: Developed interactive dashboards and backend services using ASP.NET Core, enhancing mission control simulations and educational programs.
Implemented real-time communication features with SignalR to support multi-user collaboration.
Maintained and modernized legacy interfaces, improving usability and system performance.

projects.name: Business As Usual (BAU)
projects.description: A full-scale, multi-tenant SaaS ERP platform architected with modular microservices, tenant-per-database isolation, event-driven communication, and dynamic UI composition. BAU spans 40+ business domains across Financials, HR, Operations, CRM, Inventory, Logistics, and industry-specific verticals. Features include a Module Registry Service (MRS), unified API Gateway, micro-frontend loading, and a cost-optimized AWS deployment model running under $100/month.
projects.tech: ASP.NET Core, C#, MVC, Blazor Server, Entity Framework Core, SQL Server, Docker, AWS EC2/RDS/S3/CloudFront/EventBridge, GitHub Actions CI/CD, Serilog, Nginx, Native Android & iOS clients.

projects.name: NEXTRiff
projects.description: A modular music tab creation and playback platform featuring real-time waveform visualization, tab parsing, metadata extraction, and cloud asset management. Designed with a React/TypeScript frontend and a FastAPI backend structured for iterative delivery and future extensibility.
projects.tech: FastAPI, Python, SQLModel, React/TypeScript, Cloudinary, modular API design, component-driven UI.
